title: Junior Technical Architect
blurb: Oracle data warehousing, SQL scripting and BI reporting; built automation for daily data-warehouse operations.
lead: Cut my teeth on Oracle, SQL*Plus and Cognos — wiring up the plumbing of a data warehouse and learning how data actually flows through a business.
bullets: Daily and weekly Oracle data loads

title: Senior Developer
blurb: Full SDLC on thermal-efficiency software running in 10 coal-fired power stations. VB3→VB6 upgrade, Excel integration, early GSM R&D.
lead: Led a small team modernising a Power Station Thermal Efficiency System — the work earned Meitron Eskom's Gold Plant Performance Award.
bullets: Upgraded Thermal Efficiency System VB3 → VB6
R&D for a GSM text-messaging system
Led a dev team of three
Eskom Gold Plant Performance Award (10 stations)

title: Senior Systems Analyst
blurb: Maintained and evolved three systems across Nielsen's Retail and Advertising divisions; led client consultations for new deliverables.
lead: Owned analysis, design and development across three systems in Retail and Advertising — the first of two tours at Nielsen.

title: Senior Systems Analyst
blurb: Customer requirements, solution proposals and hands-on database design across SQL CE / SQL Server 2000 & 2005. Authored team dev standards.
lead: Moved into a broader solutioning role — spec, propose, build — and wrote the standards & practices the dev team worked to.
bullets: DB design on SQL CE / SQL Server 2000 & 2005

title: Senior Systems Analyst
blurb: Brief return to Nielsen SA — cut short when Nielsen International restructured in-house IT globally.
lead: A short second stint. Contacted and convinced by Nielsen SA to rejoin the team I'd left a year earlier — international had other plans for in-house IT.
bullets: Role ended with global IT restructure

title: Head of Professional Services
blurb: Led scoping and delivery across web, mobile and social. Sat on NXT & TBWA/Tequila ops committees; work won IAC awards.
lead: Stepped up to run Professional Services — scoping, pricing and delivering web, mobile and social work; two projects took IAC awards.
bullets: IAC — Best Bank Integrated Ad Campaign

title: Business Analyst
blurb: Requirements-to-delivery across distributed and co-sourced international dev teams. Process modelling, agile PM, dev-team management.
lead: Bridged business and engineering across geographically distributed and sometimes co-sourced teams — translating requirements into shippable work.

title: BA / Software Development Manager
blurb: Led 11 developers (7 offshore). Analysis, prioritisation, QA and strategic tech roadmapping for the business.
lead: Managed an eleven-person team, set the technology roadmap, and made sure privacy, security and strategic fit stayed on the table alongside delivery.
bullets: Evaluate new tech & systems for fit
Support the mgmt team with tech insight

title: Chief Technology Officer
blurb: Aligned engineering with business objectives. Adopted DORA, ran company-wide OKRs, owned team development, training and recruiting.
lead: Promoted into the CTO seat — aligning software engineering to business objectives, tracking the horizon, and pulling DORA and company-wide OKRs into the operating model.
bullets: Track tech trends against company goals

title: Senior Business Analyst
blurb: Requirements, functional specs and cross-discipline collaboration as Scrum Master on delivery teams.
lead: Hands-on BA and Scrum Master — requirements through functional specs, working across disciplines to ship.

title: Senior Business Analyst / Team Lead
blurb: Client liaison, SoWs, budgets and forecasting while leading a 6-engineer team. Requirements, specs, UX/UI, delivery and scrum mastery.
lead: Wore the BA, team-lead and scrum-master hats at once — client liaison and SoWs up front, delivery management and UX/UI through to the finish line.
